---
title: "Interactive explainers"
order: 2
comments: false
---

A collection of self-contained, interactive visualisations for the key concepts in the course.
Each one runs entirely in your browser — drag the sliders, press the presets, and watch the maths update in real time.
They are also linked from the relevant session.

::: {.callout-tip}
These complement the sessions; they don't replace them. Use them to build intuition before or after working through each practical. Everything runs offline in the page — nothing is sent anywhere.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.iv-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(255px,1fr));gap:16px;margin:18px 0 26px}
.iv-card{border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:12px;padding:15px 17px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:9px}
.iv-card h3{margin:0;font-size:16.5px;line-height:1.25}
.iv-card .iv-desc{font-size:13.5px;flex:1;opacity:.85;line-height:1.45}
.iv-card .iv-links{display:flex;gap:12px;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;font-size:13px}
.iv-open{font-weight:600;text-decoration:none !important;background:#2563eb;color:#fff !important;padding:5px 12px;border-radius:7px}
.iv-open:hover{background:#1d4ed8}
</style>
```

## Day 1 · Delays and the reproduction number

```{=html}
<div class="iv-grid">
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Delay distributions</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Turn a continuous delay distribution into a discrete daily PMF, and see why double interval censoring shifts it.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/delay-distributions.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/delay-distributions.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Biases in delay distributions</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Watch right truncation bias the observed delays short in real time, and the knock-on dip it leaves in recent data.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/biases-in-delay-distributions.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/biases-in-delay-distributions.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Convolutions</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Slide, multiply, sum: see how latent infections become observed onsets through the delay distribution.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/convolutions.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/using-delay-distributions-to-model-the-data-generating-process-of-an-epidemic.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>The renewal equation</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Drive an epidemic with R(t) and a generation interval, and watch it turn over exactly when R crosses 1.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/renewal-equation.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/R-estimation-and-the-renewal-equation.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Day 2 · Nowcasting and forecasting

```{=html}
<div class="iv-grid">
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Nowcasting</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">See the right-edge dip from reporting delays, the reporting triangle, and how a nowcast corrects it.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/nowcasting.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/nowcasting.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Forecasting concepts</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Extend a model into the future and watch the uncertainty fan grow with the forecast horizon.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/forecasting-concepts.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/forecasting-concepts.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Forecast evaluation</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">CRPS, the PIT histogram, DSS, WIS, interval coverage, and natural-vs-log-scale scoring — how to score probabilistic forecasts, broken into components.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/forecast-evaluation.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/forecast-evaluation.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Forecasting models</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Compare random-walk, statistical and mechanistic forecasts agreeing near-term and diverging across the horizon.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/forecasting-models.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/forecasting-models.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Day 3 · Ensembles

```{=html}
<div class="iv-grid">
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Forecast ensembles</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Linear opinion pool vs quantile (Vincent) averaging — averaging probabilities vertically vs quantiles horizontally.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/forecast-ensembles.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/forecast-ensembles.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Foundations

```{=html}
<div class="iv-grid">
  <div class="iv-card">
    <h3>Bayesian updating</h3>
    <div class="iv-desc">Posterior ∝ prior × likelihood — watch the posterior form as a compromise between prior belief and data, and the data take over as they accumulate.</div>
    <div class="iv-links"><a class="iv-open" href="../interactive/bayesian-updating.html" target="_blank">Open ↗</a><a href="../sessions/delay-distributions.html">session →</a></div>
  </div>
</div>
```
